title: BTC miners end 2024 on a bubbly high, but uncertainty awaits
text: Bitcoin miners concluded 2024 on a strong note due to price appreciation, though the outlook remains uncertain. Mining difficulty has surged dramatically—rising one-third since Trump's November election and doubling year-over-year—while block rewards halved from 6.25 to 3.125 BTC in April 2024. Key Industry Trends: HODL Strategy & Debt Accumulation: Major miners including Marathon Digital, Riot Platforms, and CleanSpark adopted aggressive buy-and-hold approaches, taking on substantial debt to accumulate additional tokens. Marathon's recent purchase exemplifies the risk: the company raised $1.925 billion to acquire 15,574 BTC at approximately $98,529 each—currently underwater by roughly $54.5 million. AI Competition: The industry faces intensifying competition for energy resources. As one executive noted, right now from an economics perspective, it's going to be AI, reflecting Bitcoin mining's disadvantage against AI data centers offering actual functional value. Diversification Efforts: Miners increasingly pivot toward AI operations. Core Scientific projects a relatively equal split in revenue between AI and BTC mining by 2025, while TeraWulf leased 70+ megawatts to an AI customer for potentially over $1 billion in decade-long revenue. HUT 8's Position: HUT 8 self-mined 89 BTC in December, purchasing an additional 990 tokens at $101,710 each. The company holds 10,096 BTC total with lower average costs due to historically mined holdings.
